scenarios.title: Internal-only: duplication checks organizational memory
scenarios.description: Breadcrumbs supplies organizational research memory only; general literature research remains with the host agent.
preconditions: The graph holds a confirmed finding matching the hypothesis
steps: Call check_duplication with a hypothesis that matches an existing internal finding
Inspect the returned fields and named sources
expected: A verdict of 'match' is returned, citing the internal finding
Only the internal Breadcrumbs graph is represented in the response

scenarios.title: The save: an abandoned attempt is surfaced before a researcher repeats it
scenarios.description: This is the demo's punchline and the reason the product exists. A researcher asks a question adjacent to work a teammate already abandoned; the abandoned attempt comes back with its reason, so the researcher does not burn a week rediscovering the same dead end.
preconditions: F-093 (abandoned, stage-adjustment collapse) is seeded in the graph
steps: From a fresh session with no shared context, ask a question related to the signature F-093 tested
Call recall_findings
expected: F-093 is returned and clearly flagged as abandoned
Its reason — the effect collapsed after stage adjustment — is attached
It is not buried beneath confirmed findings merely for being abandoned

scenarios.title: The system never claims novelty
scenarios.description: Calibration is the credibility of the whole product. Claiming novelty is a claim the system cannot support, and one overclaim on stage costs more than any missing feature.
steps: Call check_duplication with a hypothesis that has no internal or external match
Inspect every user-facing string in the response
expected: The response says no prior work was found in the named sources
The sources actually searched are named
The word 'novel' does not appear anywhere in the output

scenarios.title: The internal-memory check has no literature-service dependency
scenarios.description: Breadcrumbs does not perform general literature research, so its internal-memory path has no literature-service dependency.
steps: Run check_duplication with no network
Inspect the result and graph schema
expected: The result reports only the internal graph
No external literature cache table or client is required

scenarios.title: A surprising interaction becomes approved organizational knowledge
scenarios.description: A researcher states a correction or decision naturally. The host infers one concise knowledge candidate, and Breadcrumbs supplies the exact source evidence, before/after context, and reproducible elicitation protocol needed to quantify and review it. Only the researcher's approved scientific version is persisted.
preconditions: The host can pass the relevant recent conversation turns to the MCP tool
steps: Call prepare_memory_diff with the host-inferred proposition, rationale, scope, optional kind, and exact relevant live turns
Run the returned fixed-label prior and posterior elicitation packets for the specified replicate count, then call score_surprise with those generated samples
Show the proposition, exact quote, action change, and calculated metrics to the researcher
After explicit approval, call write_knowledge
Recall it in a fresh session using its topic and scope
expected: The researcher is never asked to sync, transcribe, identify, or quote the thread, or to provide belief samples, model metadata, or a run ID
The exact live turns are content-addressed once, and the prior context excludes the selected evidence while the posterior context includes it
The score is reproducible from the stored samples
The stored metrics are server-calculated rather than caller-supplied
Recall returns the approved item with source provenance even when the fresh host adds an unknown inferred scope key
A numeric query value satisfying a stored typed condition is reported as compatible

scenarios.title: A later correction supersedes but does not erase prior knowledge
scenarios.description: Continual organizational memory is an append-only patch sequence: a newly approved belief revision points to the prior item rather than overwriting its evidence.
preconditions: An approved knowledge item already exists
steps: Approve a belief_revision with supersedes_id pointing at the prior item
Recall active knowledge
Recall again with include_superseded enabled
expected: Default recall returns only the new active patch
Audit recall returns both versions and their link

scenarios.title: A fresh agent retrieves a differently worded scoped decision
scenarios.description: The host paraphrases an approved decision and invents a plausible method key while asking about pH 6.5. Dense retrieval must bridge the wording change, and unknown inferred scope must not erase the relevant patch.
preconditions: An approved synthetic decision is indexed by FTS5 and the pinned local embedding model
steps: From a fresh host, call recall_knowledge with the assay, buffer_pH 6.5, and an unstored method key
Inspect the retrieval components and scope compatibility
expected: The active patch is returned from local retrieval
buffer_pH is compatible and method is unknown rather than exclusionary
The result exposes its BM25, dense, rank-fusion, token, and applicability components

scenarios.title: A fresh agent identifies demonstrated topic expertise with evidence
scenarios.description: The host asks who has expertise in or is investigating a topic. Breadcrumbs aggregates authored decisions, findings, and abandoned work across independent sessions while separately surfacing named session owners, without turning reviewer activity, a single question, or message volume into an unsupported expert claim.
preconditions: Canonical provisional people have source-linked contributions and named research sessions
steps: Call find_experts with a natural-language topic and optional scope
Inspect the ranked people, confidence, score components, and cited evidence
expected: The person with stronger independent primary evidence ranks first
Named relevant session owners appear separately as active investigators even without primary evidence
Investigation activity alone never creates a demonstrated expert
Repeated work in one session is capped and review-only or non-person identities are excluded
Explicit disease scope excludes off-disease findings and low-coverage incidental matches
Abandoned work is retained as evidence
The response is concise, limited to stored evidence, and makes no organizational-title or unsupported biological claim

scenarios.title: A natural correction is captured and yields a reproducible Memory Diff packet
scenarios.description: The host converts ordinary scientific dialogue into a reviewable candidate without turning provenance plumbing into a researcher form.
preconditions: The correction and its relevant context are visible to the host agent
steps: Infer one candidate proposition, rationale, and scope from the conversation
Call prepare_memory_diff with exact relevant live turns but without source identifiers, a quote, samples, a model, or a run ID
Repeat the same call and inspect the selected source, alternatives, and elicitation packets
Inspect the database before any approval write
expected: Both calls reuse one content-addressed source snapshot and return the same exact source span, deterministic run ID, and bounded candidate ordering
The returned prior packet excludes the selected evidence and the posterior packet adds it while keeping the proposition and fixed labels identical
The result contains source provenance, an authenticated actor hint when available or an explicit missing-author field otherwise, and a partial write template but no fabricated judgments
Preparation stores only raw source provenance; no knowledge row is created before explicit approval
